activities_projects.title: Critical Analysis of the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the poem’s themes, moral lessons, and narrative style
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the main moral and the story’s narrative style
Explain how the poet uses humor and exaggeration to teach a lesson
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical interpretation and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and textual support (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Symbolism and Allegory
activities_projects.objective: Understand symbolic meanings and allegorical elements
steps: Identify symbols such as the cakes, the woodpecker, and Saint Peter
Explain how each symbol conveys moral and cultural values
Discuss how allegory reinforces the poem’s lesson
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands symbolic and allegorical elements in literature

activities_projects.title: Character Motivation Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze why characters act as they do
steps: Examine the woman’s behavior and Saint Peter’s response
Explain their motivations and consequences
Discuss how these actions reflect universal human traits
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops understanding of character motivation and moral reasoning

activities_projects.title: Literary Device Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Identify advanced literary devices and their effects
steps: Find examples of rhyme, repetition, exaggeration, and imagery
Explain how these devices enhance humor, mood, and meaning
Provide line references for each example
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes advanced poetic techniques and their impact

activities_projects.title: Comparative Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Compare this poem with other folk tales or legends
steps: Think of a folk tale with a moral lesson
Compare themes, characters, and narrative techniques
Write similarities and differences in a table

activities_projects.title: Perspective Writing
steps: Imagine you are the woman before being transformed
Write a diary entry explaining your thoughts and feelings
Include reflections on generosity, selfishness, and consequences

activities_projects.title: Philosophical Interpretation
activities_projects.objective: Explore deeper moral and ethical ideas
steps: Reflect on why selfishness is condemned in the poem
Discuss how the poem connects to human values and ethics
Support your ideas with specific lines from the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking and moral reasoning
activities_projects.evaluation: Insightful explanation with textual evidence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Story Expansion
steps: Write a continuation of the poem’s story from the woodpecker’s point of view
Include challenges she faces and lessons learned
Use descriptive language and dialogue
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances creative and narrative writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, structure, and language use (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Real-Life Application Project
activities_projects.objective: Connect the poem’s moral to real-life practices
steps: Research cultural or historical stories that teach generosity or consequences of selfishness
Write a short report linking them to the poem
Explain how the lesson is still relevant today
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects literature to real-life ethics and culture
activities_projects.evaluation: Research quality, clarity, and relevance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Discussion and Debate
steps: Debate whether Saint Peter’s punishment was fair or harsh
Discuss different viewpoints in class
Write a summary of key points and personal opinion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical reasoning and communication skills
